skill.name: react-modernization
skill.description: Modernize React applications by planning version upgrades, converting class components to hooks, adopting React 18 patterns, and applying codemods.
skill.summary: Guide React upgrades, hooks migrations, concurrent feature adoption, and codemod-based refactoring.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk patterns, but the weak-crypto, backtick execution, dynamic import, network, and reconnaissance hits are documentation examples in SKILL.md. The meaningful residual risk is that the skill recommends npm and npx codemod commands that can execute third-party packages and modify project files, so publication is acceptable with user caution.

medium_findings.title: Codemod Commands Can Execute External Packages
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends npm install and npx codemod commands. These commands are legitimate for React migrations, but they can execute packages from the JavaScript ecosystem and rewrite project files, so users should review package sources and run them in version-controlled branches.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and are intended migration tooling, not hidden execution. Risk is medium because npm and npx package execution can affect the local project if used without review.

low_findings.title: Documentation Fetch Examples Are Not Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The fetch calls are local application examples showing lifecycle-to-hook migration. They use relative API paths and do not send credentials or data to an external domain.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both fetch calls are inside illustrative React component examples and use relative paths. No evidence found of external network destinations or credential handling.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are React Code Splitting Examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import expressions are examples for React lazy loading. They import local components and are not part of executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports appear inside a Markdown JavaScript example under Code Splitting. No evidence found that the skill dynamically loads untrusted code at runtime.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Pattern Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm, system reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick execution hits align with prose, JSX property access, and Markdown code fence syntax. No evidence found of cryptographic code, reconnaissance commands, or Ruby shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Markdown documentation and React examples at the cited locations. The static labels do not match executable behavior in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes npm and npx commands for codemod tooling. This is expected for React modernization, but agents should ask before executing package installs or codemods and should keep changes under version control.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and relevant to the skill purpose. The risk depends on user execution and package trust, not on hidden malicious skill behavior.

content.value_statement: React upgrades often mix framework changes, component rewrites, and testing risk. This skill gives structured migration guidance for hooks, React 18 features, TypeScript, performance work, and codemods.
actual_capabilities: Plans React 16, 17, and 18 upgrade paths with key breaking changes.
Shows how to convert class state, lifecycle methods, context, and HOCs to hooks.
Explains React 18 APIs including createRoot, automatic batching, transitions, and Suspense.
Provides codemod command examples for JSX transform and unsafe lifecycle migrations.
Guides TypeScript typing for common React component patterns.
Lists migration checks for testing, performance, and incremental rollout.
limitations: It is guidance-only and does not include executable scripts in this skill package.
Codemod examples may need package and version review before use.
It does not cover every framework integration, routing library, or state management library.
It cannot verify runtime behavior without the target project and test suite.

use_cases.title: Upgrade a Legacy React App
use_cases.description: Plan a staged React 16 to 18 migration with dependency updates, root API changes, and test checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Convert Class Components to Hooks
use_cases.description: Translate class state, lifecycle methods, context consumers, and HOCs into functional component patterns.

use_cases.title: Adopt Modern Performance Patterns
use_cases.description: Evaluate useMemo, useCallback, React.memo, code splitting, transitions, and Suspense for targeted improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Upgrade Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner assessment for an existing project
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React project and identify the safest upgrade path. List current version risks, required dependency changes, and tests to run first.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this class component to a functional component with hooks. Preserve behavior and explain each lifecycle replacement.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Codemod Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend codemods for this React migration. Explain what each command changes, what to review manually, and how to validate the diff.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Migration Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased React modernization plan for this codebase. Include version upgrades, hooks migration, TypeScript adoption, performance work, rollback points, and test gates.

output_examples.input: A team needs to move a React 17 app to React 18.
output_examples.output: A phased migration plan covering dependency updates, createRoot adoption, StrictMode testing, concurrent rendering checks, and release validation.

output_examples.input: A class component uses state, componentDidMount, and componentWillUnmount.
output_examples.output: A hooks migration outline that maps state to useState, side effects to useEffect, and cleanup logic to the returned effect function.

output_examples.input: A large app has slow route loading and expensive list rendering.
output_examples.output: A performance review plan using route-level code splitting, memoized calculations, stable callbacks, and measurement before and after changes.

best_practices: Upgrade incrementally and keep each phase small enough to review.
Run codemods on a clean branch and inspect every generated diff.
Use tests, StrictMode, and performance measurements before declaring success.
anti_patterns: Do not convert all components at once without test coverage.
Do not add useMemo or useCallback without a measured need.
Do not run third-party codemods without reviewing source, versions, and expected changes.

faq.question: Can this skill upgrade my app automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides migration guidance and command examples, but project changes still need review and testing.

faq.question: Does it support React 18?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers createRoot, automatic batching, transitions, Suspense, and StrictMode considerations.

faq.question: Can it help with class components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to map state, lifecycle methods, context, and HOCs to hooks.

faq.question: Are the codemod commands safe to run?
faq.answer: They are standard migration patterns, but they execute packages and modify files. Review packages and run them on a clean branch.

faq.question: Does it include TypeScript migration help?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for typed props, ReactNode children, and generic component patterns.

faq.question: What should I test after migration?
faq.answer: Run unit, integration, and browser tests. Also check console warnings, StrictMode behavior, and key performance paths.
